256.name: Chilling Cell
256.description: The cold, uncomfortable stone which forms this cell absorbs little of the warmth of the outside hall, keeping its occupant constantly shivering. A roughly cut tunnel no more than a few inches wide leads back up to the surface, providing barely enough ventilation to allow normal breathing.

npcs.name: Elven prisoner
npcs.description: He begins babbling in terror as you enter his cell, tearing at his hair and howling in anguish at the sight of some imaginary fiend.

npcs.name: Ragged prisoner
npcs.description: the prisoner's miserable condition.  His voice is strong and melodious, and his posture is one of quiet pride.

257.name: Dark Aisle
257.description: This small little aisleway leading off from junction is unnaturally quiet. Your mind recoils from the oppressive silence, and you long for the drip of water that once annoyed you.

258.name: Square Room
258.description: This room is bereft of ornamentation, save for a single iron brazier burning smokily in one corner. A thin layer of dust coats the floor, muffling the sound of your footfalls slightly. By cocking your head to one side and listening carefully, you can just barely make out the sound of steel ringing on steel.

259.name: Sitting Room
259.description: The silence is tangible as you step into this small sitting room; it's almost as if you've entered a sanctuary where all sound has been expunged. A series of plain wooden benches with thick iron bands have been bolted to the north wall, where a few men in deeply cowled robes sit quietly.

npcs.name: Cowled monk
npcs.description: features are visible.  But from what meager bit you can see, it is easy to discern that he is powerfully built and will tolerate no foolishness.

260.name: Hall of Truth
260.description: You stand on an iron catwalk high above the floor below, where scores of robed men can be seen training in perfect unison under the watchful eyes of their masters. Striding forward purposefully, you reach the end of the walkway and step onto a wooden platform that extends from the wall.

npcs.name: Robed monk
npcs.description: to leave his post.  Whoever trained him must have instilled a strong

261.name: Stone Cell
261.description: The archway from which you entered disappears with a flash of incandescent light as you step into the tiny stone room, which is completely unadorned except for an obscure symbol carved into the southern wall. The symbol appears to be a stylized representation of a human hand.

244.name: Dark Corridor
244.description: Occasional drips of water from above slide lazily down the overhanging stones to the ground in a quiet patter, distracting you as you ponder the wiseness of wandering around in the bowels of the temple. A rough stairway climbs up to the ground level, and the narrow passageway continues to the north.

245.name: Dark Corridor
245.description: Squatting on your knees in the low passageway, you carefully move along its edges. Rocky protrusions in the walls catch at your clothing and scrape at your skin, and the water drips steadily from above. Withered doors of wood flank the passage on either side, and an occasional scream of terror can be heard in the darkness.

246.name: Dungeon Cell
246.description: This little cell features all of the usual conveniences: rats, spiders, a grimy straw pallet, and a steady drip of water from above. An extended stay here would be uncomfortable to say the least.

247.name: Dungeon Cell
247.description: A lumpy pallet of straw is crumpled in one corner of this tiny cell, where a few emaciated rats chew halfheartedly at the material. The ever-present stream of water pours down from above, soaking the ground and chilling the air. A decidedly unpleasant place.

248.name: Dark Corridor
248.description: This part of the corridor is slighty more pleasant; the incessant dripping of water has somehow been halted here. A heavy chair of iron is bolted to the rock face, perhaps for a jailer or guard. The corridor continues to the north and south.

npcs.name: Unshaven jailer
npcs.description: surprised if someone told you he'd just been pulled out of one of the cells a week ago and put on duty.

249.name: Subterranean Junction
249.description: Three passageways meet in a triple junction here, leading off to the west, east, and south. All directions are shrouded in darkness, and the quiet drip of water echoes from somewhere to the south.

250.name: Smoky Passage
250.description: This branch of the cavern has enough furnishing to at least come close to resembling some form of civility, especially when compared with the rather spartan comfort of the dungeons. A grimy expanse of greenish carpeting wanders over the gritty floor, and a few brands which have been dipped in pitch give off a smoky glow from iron rungs set into the walls.

251.name: Smoky Passage
251.description: A pall of choking smoke hangs thick in the air here, making breathing a chore. A welcome breeze of fresh air drifts in from the north, futilely attempting to dissipate the greasy smoke. Rubbing at red-rimmed eyes and dashing away the tears that have begun to form, you ponder your options.

npcs.name: Indulgent priest
npcs.description: carefully, and he shifts his bulk about nervously.  Droplets of sweat begin sliding down his greasy face, and his mouth starts working soundlessly.

253.name: Rolim's Quarters
253.description: Bulky, squarish furniture cut from heavy marble and dark wood dominates this room, accented by an occasional stretch of gold leaf. While the motif must have cost a fortune, it certainly doesn't seem to be aesthetically pleasing. A solitary door allows exit.

npcs.name: Rolim
npcs.description: interrupted, what gall.

254.name: Darial's Quarters
254.description: Peerless craftsmanship surrounds you; every table, chair, and desk is covered with unparalleled woodcuts and precise etching. A beautiful vase positioned immediately next to the door must have cost millions of obsidian alone, not to mention the fabulous tapestries that carpet the walls. How did a simple priest manage to afford such lavish furnishings?

npcs.name: Darial
npcs.description: to rectify your behavior.

255.name: Plebian Passage
255.description: There's really not much to this place. A pair of lamps hanging from iron chains provide enough illumination to see a few feet in front of you, and a series of iron-banded doors flank the passage.

270.name: Deep in the Pool
270.description: The pressure is significantly greater at this depth, almost enough to cause pain. Shattered bits of iron lay everywhere; how they ended up in such a haphazard pattern is a mystery which would certainly be long in the solving. the trapdoor.

243.name: Crude Stairway
243.description: A series of roughly chiseled, stone steps lead down into darkness. Whoever built this stairway must have needed it in a hurry, as there is little beauty in its design. On the other hand, the average temple-goer was probably not supposed to be here. Now that you think about it, what are YOU doing here?

262.name: Circular Landing
262.description: You find yourself in a circular room with no apparent exits except for an opening in what appears to be, by your best reckoning, the northern area. The walls and floor are constructed of an aqua colored stone that glows slightly with inner light, and no ceiling is visible above you.

263.name: Chamber of Stratos
263.description: Powerful gusts of wind race about this chamber, rebounding against the walls and buffeting you in forceful torrents. Small, indistinct figures are barely visible in the whipping windstorm, always just out of focus. You feel somewhat dizzy and confused.

npcs.name: Essence of stratos
npcs.description: harmed by a weapon, or perhaps to rend your flesh. . .

264.name: Silent Chamber
264.description: The air is still and deathly quiet in this round chamber; your footfalls seem unbearably loud as you step forward to survey the area. Painstakingly smoothed walls surround you, and the only visible exit is to the east. You are carrying: *a component pouch*

npcs.name: Beast
npcs.description: scratch the floor as it stretches in its sleep.  One hairy paw is

265.name: Silent Chamber
265.description: Like the room to the west, this chamber is entirely devoid of sound. A high wall of rough granite rises above you to the north, and to the south a dark pool beckons you forward. You have lost 2550 experience.

npcs.name: Beast
npcs.description: scratch the floor as it stretches in its sleep.  One hairy paw is

266.name: Ceremonial Grandroom
266.description: Priceless crimson carpeting covers the floor of this magnificent room, which you can only assume to be the largest in the realm. A narrow hallway to the south leads away from the grandroom, and a fabulous chandelier hangs to the east.

267.name: Stillwater Pool
267.description: You peer about the pool, vainly trying to brush away the silt which hangs thick in the motionless water. Your equipment is soon clogged with dirt and debris, and lifting a foot out of the sucking mud becomes an immense effort. A craggy series of rock formations aligned in such a way as to almost resemble a stairway offers egress to the north. You have lost 850 experience.

268.name: Boundless Path
268.description: There is an unnerving sense of limitlessness here; the gravelly path on which you stand extends beyond your line of sight to the east and west, and you get the distinct impression that it simply never ends. An iron doorway sits squarely north of you, always remaining directly in front no matter how far to the east or west you wander. Failing the doorway, you might risk the rather deadly looking jump to the south, back down the wall.

269.name: Stillwater Pool
269.description: You peer about the pool, vainly trying to brush away the silt which hangs thick in the motionless water. Your equipment is soon clogged with dirt and debris, and lifting a foot out of the sucking mud becomes an immense effort. A craggy series of rock formations aligned in such a way as to almost resemble a stairway offers egress to the north. You have lost 2550 experience.

271.name: Narrow Corridor
271.description: Nothing seems to follow in this place; one minute you're walking along a limitless plane, and the next, you find yourself in an oak-paneled corridor leading up to a doorway set into the north wall.

272.name: Round Chamber
272.description: Having surmounted the final obstacle, an enormous sense of accomplishment flows into you, bringing unprecedented feelings of confidence and preparedness coursing through your veins. A single opening in the chamber allows passage to the west.

273.name: Rejoining
273.description: Brilliant light fills this room, radiating from a single point high above you which shines with the fury of a thousand stars. A sense of power fills you, and it suddenly becomes clear that you have been judged worthy to serve Isvaelin.

274.name: Hall of the Masters
274.description: You stride along a broad hallway paved with silver. Scores of cowled figures shuffle past in the darkness, heads bent in concentration, while others practice hurling daggers at wooden targets set up along the walls. Most noticeable, however, are the brown-cloaked men who step lightly from place to place, bending only to give commands or to correct throwing techniques.

275.name: Hall of the Masters
275.description: A pair of wide corridors branch off on either side in this section of the Hall, leading off to other sections of the training areas. The press of the trainees lessens considerably to the north, where a mere half dozen sit in apparent meditation.

npcs.name: Initiate of the Order
npcs.description: best to appear detached as he stares at you, almost as if you were

276.name: Initiate Grounds
276.description: Assassins in training are present in profusion here, practicing martial arts under the eyes of figures in brown cloaks. An occasional cry of pain rings out as one trainee or another is judged unfit to continue. Definitely an anxious situation.

277.name: Hall of the Accepted
277.description: The training is less frenzied in this area; precious few can be seen practicing their arts compared to the desperate honing of skills displayed in the Initiate grounds. Somber faced young men walk past looking relaxed, but a certain tightness around their eyes indicates a preparedness to face any challenge.

278.name: Accepted's Chamber
278.description: Long bladed daggers seem to be everywhere in this rather restricted living area. Various dog-eared books defining proper assassination techniques and careful diagrams of the elven anatomy lay strewn about the floor, together with numerous lengths of strong cord for strangling. The room's occupant must take his work quite seriously.

npcs.name: Accepted of the Order
npcs.description: death while standing motionless, and his gaze seems to pierce your soul. Staying out of his way would seem to be the most prudent course of action.

279.name: Hall of the Accepted
279.description: Little noise can be heard in this solemn hall, which is free from the bustle of wandering Initiates. Oaken doors leading to individual quarters line the flagstone walls, and a threadbare stretch of red carpeting covers the floor.

280.name: Accepted's Chamber
280.description: A low cot of pine is tucked neatly away in the northeast corner of this tidy room, adjacent to a stack of carefully organized manuscripts and leather-bound volumes. There is a noticeable lack of illumination, and you wonder how someone could bear living in perpetual darkness.

npcs.name: Accepted of the Order
npcs.description: death while standing motionless, and his gaze seems to pierce your soul. Staying out of his way would seem to be the most prudent course of action.

281.name: Accepted's Chamber
281.description: Strangely enough, this room is completely devoid of furniture or ornamentation. A ragged length of grimy carpeting vainly seeks to cover the cold, stone floor, and a door of some undiscernable wood allows passage to the north.

npcs.name: Accepted of the Order
npcs.description: death while standing motionless, and his gaze seems to pierce your soul. Staying out of his way would seem to be the most prudent course of action.

282.name: Hall of the Masters
282.description: The training dummies and anatomical wall charts have been cleared away in this section of the Hall, to be replaced by large tapestries covered with an undecipherable, runic script. The few assassins who are present refrain from speech and stand motionless before the tapestries, poring over the flowing letters.

283.name: Before the Shrine
283.description: You stand at the shore of a dark, underground lake, preparing yourself for what you know you must do. A solitary pyramid floats on the surface by some unseen mechanism, leaving only a slight depression in the surrounding water. There is no apparent way to reach the pyramid without swimming, but you are suddenly certain that this should not concern you. The waters will not break beneath your feet, for you shall will them not to do so. . .

225.name: The Hall of Isvaelin
225.description: A hulking stone monument to Isvaelin dominates the temple's entrance, clearly created to drive a sense of awe into the common worshiper and to intimidate the less devout. Rubies the size of small oxen glitter from within sunken eye sockets, cascades of ivory scales flow to the floor to form a priceless suit of mail, and great bands of beaten gold encircle the god's wrists and ankles. Unable to pull your eyes from the towering statue, you grudgingly credit the builders for crafting such an imposing spectacle; the desire to flee its oppressive presence and the overwhelming need to fall to your knees in worship are equally overpowering.

226.name: Common Prayer Hall
226.description: You stand in the common area of the temple, where numerous languid priests listen to the pleas of elves from all parts of the world who have journeyed to the temple in hopes of having their prayers answered by the almighty Isvaelin. The temple spreads out in all directions.

npcs.name: Money collector
npcs.description: provide the temple with some money so that it may aid the poor.  Charity, he reminds you, will be well rewarded in the next world.

npcs.name: Pious elf
npcs.description: her sins to Isvaelin and begs for aid in some trivial task that she must

npcs.name: Unimportant priest
npcs.description: thoroughly just before leaving his quarters.  Jowls of fat hang from his red-hued face, and he sniffs the air as you approach.

227.name: Western Hallway
227.description: Richly woven tapestries carpet the walls of this broad hallway, apparently depicting the glories of Isvaelin and His faithful priests in battles long past. From the way the common elven soldier is ignored in the great scenes it is easy to deduce that the clergy here hold elevated opinions of themselves and their roles in inconsequential wars. Apparently the vow of humility has fallen into disuse.

228.name: Priests' Hall
228.description: This narrow but thickly carpeted hall runs north and south, with doors to the priests' individual quarters dotting the walls at regular intervals. All of doors are of uniform size and ornamentation, with the exception of one set into the wall where the hallway terminates several yards to the north.

229.name: Priests' Hall
229.description: An impressive door of lovingly crafted oak is set into the wall in front of you, signifying that its owner is a bit more important than the average priest. This thought being foremost in your mind, it is with some trepidation that you approach the end of the hall.

npcs.name: Jarlan
npcs.description: his face.  Steel cuffs encircle his thickly muscled forearms, and he lounges against the wall with the air of alertness that surrounds trained

230.name: High Priest's Chambers
230.description: Opulent furniture and priceless carpets have been positioned about the room, not at all in the plain, severe style expected of the clergy. Surely a simple churchman's salary could not pay for the luxurious furnishings which surround you as you walk down the marble steps and into the good priest's chamber.

npcs.name: Golan, the High Priest
npcs.description: preferred to be left undisturbed.

231.name: Stone Room
231.description: A plain wooden bedframe, topped by a lumpy rice mattress, takes up most of the room. A small chair and writing table stand in one corner, where a single candle burns fitfully, providing a small pool of light barely sufficient for reading.

232.name: Priests' Hall
232.description: The hallway reeks of stark regularity, relieved only by the occasional painting or tapestry. Simple priest's quarters line the west wall.

233.name: Stone Room
233.description: A crude straw pallet has been rolled up and shoved into the corner of the room to free up some space, although it's readily apparent that little room remains for even the small table, chair, and plain wooden chest, in which the room's occupant keeps his few belongings.

npcs.name: Priest of isvaelin
npcs.description: whimpering or burying his head in his hands.  He lifts his head to meet your gaze, and you cringe at the haunted look of defeat in his hazel eyes.

234.name: Priests' Hall
234.description: You have reached the end of the hall, although you'd never know it from the unbroken regularity of plain wooden doors along the west wall. The only exit lies to the north, along the hall.

235.name: Stone Room
235.description: A threadbare rug covers most of the floor here, providing a bit of relief from the cold, bleak stone which surrounds you. A cleverly crafted folding table has been stowed beneath the iron bed affixed to the wall, and a coal- filled brazier gives off a smoky glow in the corner.

npcs.name: Priest of isvaelin
npcs.description: whimpering or burying his head in his hands.  He lifts his head to meet your gaze, and you cringe at the haunted look of defeat in his hazel eyes.

236.name: East Branch
236.description: You stand on a slim, thickly woven carpet that leads away from the prayer area. Unadorned walls of grey stone flank the carpeting on either side, and iron braziers high above you provide smoky illumination. Travel is possible only to the east and west.

237.name: Ceremonial Grandroom
237.description: The temple narrows here, where a hallway to the west links the two main sections of the building. The grandroom spreads out to the east and north.

npcs.name: Unimportant priest
npcs.description: thoroughly just before leaving his quarters.  Jowls of fat hang from his red-hued face, and he sniffs the air as you approach.

238.name: Ceremonial Grandroom
238.description: The grandroom's centre is just northward of here, where a fine chandelier hangs from a slender chain. To the west, the room begins to taper slightly, narrowing into a hallway that leads back to the common areas. The simple enormity of the place is beginning to press down on you; you are feeling more insignificant each minute.

239.name: Ceremonial Grandroom
239.description: You stand in the southeast corner of the grandroom, impressed by the simple enormity of its construction. The most outstanding feature, however, is easily the crystal dome set into the ceiling to the northwest.

240.name: Ceremonial Grandroom
240.description: The size of this room is positively astounding. As you tilt your head back to admire the fine woodwork on the ceiling, you stop to marvel at what a feat of engineering this entire building is. The roof has somehow been persuaded to remain in place without benefit of buttresses.

npcs.name: Pious elf
npcs.description: her sins to Isvaelin and begs for aid in some trivial task that she must

241.name: Centre of the Grandroom
241.description: An enormous, golden chandelier hangs overhead, swinging precariously on a single gilded chain. A crystal dome high above catches the light and refracts it brilliantly at a thousand different angles, sending shards of radiant light skittering across the carpeted floor. A profound sense of holiness washes over you as you bask in the comforting glow, and for a moment your soul is at peace.

242.name: At the Altar
242.description: You stand before a vast, ivory altar. Fine scrollwork and etchings cover the block, along with flowing script written in a spidery hand. A large curtain of deep blue velvet hangs directly behind the altar, blocking your view of what lies beyond.
